attachments.content:
[Design System] This project uses the **Design System** design system. This is a binding choice for visual style — every visual must follow it. Don't invent colors, type, spacing, or components not grounded here.

Scope: the design system is a visual style reference only. Its guide may describe example products, brands, or people that are unrelated to the user and unrelated to the subject of this conversation. Never treat anything in the design system as a fact about the user, their work, or the topic they asked about.

    Explore it to find what you need:
    - Always copy out the fonts and colors you need
    - For prototypes and designs, always copy out any relevant components
    - If the design system contains existing mocks of products, and you were asked to design or prototype something similar, copy and fork those mocks to start your design. This helps you make high-quality designs.

    Explore it quickly to find relevant UI kits (e.g. mocks of existing products you can copy and fork)

Full system at `/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/`. Before producing any visuals, explore it: call `list_files("/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/")` to see the structure, then `read_file` the README/base.md or whatever index file it has. Don't guess at the design system's contents.

CSS tokens: the guide may describe tokens in prose, but the exact `--*` names are defined in the design system's stylesheet(s). Before writing any `var(--*)`, look up the real name in the design system's `.css` files (`list_files` + `read_file` under `/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/`). Never guess a token name — an unresolved `var()` silently falls back to the browser default.

For assets and UI kits beyond the guide: `read_file("/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/<path>")` and `copy_files` to bring them into the current project.
attachments.name: Design System (design system)

attachments.content:
Act as an expert Frontend Engineer and UI/UX Designer specialized in Solarpunk and regenerative mobile interfaces. Your mission is to create a fully interactive, high-fidelity React and Tailwind CSS prototype for "ABC OS" (African Business Cooperatives Operating System), based strictly on the following structural and visual specifications.

### 1. DESIGN SYSTEM & VISUAL TOKENS
- **Theme:** Deep Dark Solarpunk.
- **Colors:**
  - Background: Ultra-dark charcoal/brown-black (`bg-[#121110]` or `bg-[#181614]`).
  - Cards/Containers: Dark organic grey (`bg-[#221f1c]`).
  - Primary Accent: Living Green (`text-[#10b981]` or `bg-[#059669]`) for progress, positive metrics, and primary highlights.
  - Secondary Accent: Terracotta/Clay Orange (`bg-[#e15f41]` or `bg-[#d95436]`) for call-to-actions, active buttons, and structural emphasis.
  - Text: Off-white/Cream (`text-[#f5f2eb]`) for primary text, muted tan/grey for secondary text.
- **Typography:** Clean, premium sans-serif with high contrast and scannability.
- **Icons:** Use `lucide-react` icons exclusively.

### 2. ARCHITECTURE & NAVIGATION
The interface must be a self-contained layout representing a mobile device frame. Implement a global state `currentTab` to cycle through the screens via a persistent bottom navigation bar. 
The navigation tabs must be:
1. **Community** (Icon: Users)
2. **Learn** (Icon: GraduationCap)
3. **Build** (Icon: Wrench)
4. **Brand** (Icon: Store/Award)

*Bonus state:* Include a toggle or a button to simulate the initial minimalist **Splash Screen** ("ABC OS: De l'Apprentissage à la Coopération, de la Coopération à la Civilisation") with "Join the Cooperative" and "Sign In" buttons.

### 3. DETAILED COMPONENT SPECIFICATIONS

#### Tab 1: Community Hub
- **Header:** Title "Community" with a search bar ("Search discussions and members...") and a notification bell icon.
- **Sub-navigation pills:** "Feed" (Active with underline), "Co-ops", "Events".
- **Feed Stream (Cards):**
  - Card 1: User "Kaelan Patel" (2h ago). Text: "Looking for advice from anyone who has gone through the process. What are the key documents and registrations required? #Legal #Startup". Footer with Like (12), Comment (5), and Share interactions.
  - Card 2: User "Amara Okoro" (1 day ago). Text: "Our Agri-Tech coop just secured its first round of funding! A huge thank you to everyone in the #BuildHub for the feedback on our pitch deck...". Include a beautiful placeholder container representing a green agricultural crop field. Footer with Like (78), Comment (23).
- **Floating Action Button (FAB):** A fixed terracotta circular button with a "+" icon at the bottom right.

#### Tab 2: Learn Hub
- **Header:** Title "Learn Hub" with a search bar ("Search courses & topics...").
- **Section 1: "My Courses"** (Horizontal scrolling row or grid)
  - Card "Architect Principles" with a bright green progress bar filled at ~60%.
  - Card "Financial Literacy" with a green progress bar filled at ~30%.
- **Section 2: "Recommended for You"**
  - Card "System Building" (Sub-label: Cooperative Foundations) with an artisan/weaving background placeholder.
  - Card "Brand Storytelling" (Sub-label: Marketing & Comm).
- **Section 3: "Course Categories"** (2-column clean grid with subtle borders and custom icons)
  - Architect Principles (Icon: Layers)
  - System Building (Icon: Users)
  - Brand Storytelling (Icon: Megaphone)
  - Financial Literacy (Icon: Landmark)
  - Sustainable Practices (Icon: Leaf)
  - Solarpunk Principles (Icon: Sun)

#### Tab 3: Build Hub
- **Header:** Title "Build Hub" with two top tabs: "My Projects" (Active) and "Cooperative Tools".
- **Project Cards (Vertical Stack):**
  - **Card 1: Solaris Agri-Coop**
    - High-quality image placeholder of solar panels in a green field.
    - Status badge: "In Progress" (Green pill).
    - Milestone Progress text: "3 of 5" with a matching linear green progress bar.
    - Next step label: "Next: Finalize Distribution Plan".
    - Team layout: Overlapping avatar components (+2 members).
  - **Card 2: Umoja Weavers Collective**
    - Image placeholder of a community/agricultural garden.
    - Status badge: "Planning" (Blue/Muted pill).
    - Milestone Progress text: "1 of 8" with a linear progress bar.
    - Next step label: "Next: Draft Cooperative Agreement".
    - Team layout: Overlapping avatars.
- **Call-to-Action Card:** Dotted border container at the bottom with a central "+" icon. Text: "Ready to build together? Tap the '+' button to start your first cooperative business and bring your vision to life."

#### Tab 4: Brand Hub
- **Header:** Title "Brand Hub".
- **Metric Showcase:** A large, beautiful radial circular chart displaying the **"Brand Impact Score"** centered with the number **85 / 100**. Subtitle: "Your current community resonance."
- **Action List Rows** (Clean clickable items with right chevrons):
  - Row 1: Icon: BookOpen | Title: "Crafting Your Story" | Subtitle: "Define values, mission, and visual identity."
  - Row 2: Icon: DollarSign | Title: "Monetization Strategies" | Subtitle: "Explore pricing, digital products, and grants."
  - Row 3: Icon: Users | Title: "Community Impact" | Subtitle: "Track social good and member collaboration."
- **Section: "Inspiration Hub"**
  - Horizontal card: "Soko Weavers Doubled Their Reach - How cooperative marketing expanded their global footprint."

### 4. TECHNICAL CONSTRAINTS
- Return a single, cohesive, self-contained React component.
- Ensure all states (switching tabs, clicking actions) work seamlessly to make the prototype completely interactive.
- Use Tailwind classes cleanly, avoiding external CSS files.
- Ensure the container mimics a beautiful mobile view viewport with proper padding, smooth rounded corners, and a realistic layout shadow.
attachments.name: Pasted text (81 lines)

content.content:
Now let me build the icons module — a tiny lucide-compatible SVG component set with all icons we need.ABC OS prototype is live. The mobile frame holds all four tabs (Community, Learn, Build, Brand) plus the splash screen, wired through a floating bottom nav.

**What's in there**
- **Splash** — sun-and-leaf mark, the trilingual tagline in Instrument Serif, two CTAs.
- **Community Hub** — search + bell, Feed/Co-ops/Events pills, Kaelan & Amara cards (Amara's field rendered as a CSS-painted crop terrace), terracotta FAB.
- **Learn Hub** — horizontally-scrolling "My Courses" with progress bars, "Recommended" cards with woven/garden textures, 6-tile category grid.
- **Build Hub** — segmented "My Projects / Cooperative Tools" tabs, Solaris (solar+field) and Umoja (garden) project cards with stacked avatars, dotted-border CTA. Switch to "Cooperative Tools" for a 6-tile grid.
- **Brand Hub** — radial score with gradient ring + tick marks, sub-stats (Story/Reach/Impact), three action rows, Soko Weavers inspiration card + Brand Atlas row.

**Tweaks** (toolbar toggle) lets you flip the splash on, jump between tabs, and switch phone size. Splash and tab state persist via the host.

Next iterations I can layer in if useful: hook the accent-color tweak to live styling, build a project-detail screen when you tap a card, add a "+" sheet for the FAB.

ABC OS prototype is live. The mobile frame holds all four tabs (Community, Learn, Build, Brand) plus the splash screen, wired through a floating bottom nav.

**What's in there**
- **Splash** — sun-and-leaf mark, the trilingual tagline in Instrument Serif, two CTAs.
- **Community Hub** — search + bell, Feed/Co-ops/Events pills, Kaelan & Amara cards (Amara's field rendered as a CSS-painted crop terrace), terracotta FAB.
- **Learn Hub** — horizontally-scrolling "My Courses" with progress bars, "Recommended" cards with woven/garden textures, 6-tile category grid.
- **Build Hub** — segmented "My Projects / Cooperative Tools" tabs, Solaris (solar+field) and Umoja (garden) project cards with stacked avatars, dotted-border CTA. Switch to "Cooperative Tools" for a 6-tile grid.
- **Brand Hub** — radial score with gradient ring + tick marks, sub-stats (Story/Reach/Impact), three action rows, Soko Weavers inspiration card + Brand Atlas row.

**Tweaks** (toolbar toggle) lets you flip the splash on, jump between tabs, and switch phone size. Splash and tab state persist via the host.

Next iterations I can layer in if useful: hook the accent-color tweak to live styling, build a project-detail screen when you tap a card, add a "+" sheet for the FAB.

content.content: Utilise l'app que tu as crée comme base de refference du Contexte et embed le dans un Landing page Attirant et claire pour ABC OS

Landing page livrée — **Landing.html**.

**Ce qu'il contient**
- **TopNav** glassy collante avec logo soleil-et-feuille
- **Hero éditorial** — manchette serif géante en trois lignes, sous-titre, CTA terracotta pulsant + bouton fantôme, ligne de social proof. Le téléphone à droite **fait défiler automatiquement** les 4 hubs toutes les ~5s et porte trois call-outs flottants connectés par des pointillés (Brand Impact 85/100, jalon Solaris validé, +128 membres).
- **Marquee** des coopératives membres
- **Manifeste** en grand format avec emphase italique sur "coopération" et "civilisation"
- **Les quatre piliers** — 4 cartes Community/Learn/Build/Brand avec icône, nom français, description, bullets
- **Phone Tour** — les 4 hubs côte à côte en téléphones miniatures interactifs (hover pour soulever)
- **Stats** — 6 chiffres en grand serif
- **Programme — Trois actes** : Apprendre / Coopérer / Civiliser, chaque carte coiffée d'une texture (weave, garden, solar)
- **Témoignages** — une grande citation Umoja Weavers + deux plus petites
- **FAQ** accordéon
- **CTA Band** avec soleil rayonnant en arrière-plan et formulaire d'inscription
- **Footer** complet

L'app `ABC OS.html` reste accessible séparément ; la landing en réutilise les composants pour que les écrans embarqués soient strictement identiques au prototype.
